source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals

confidence_rationale: Direct hippocampal place-cell recording in freely-moving rats showing forward-trajectory sequences predicting future goal-directed paths. Provides one of the substrate candidates for behavioural diversity generation -- hippocampal forward sequences as the option-proposal mechanism. Already cross-referenced by REE substrates MECH-292 ranked ghost-goal bank, MECH-293 awake ghost-goal probes; this paper grounds the relevance to ARC-065 specifically.
failure_signatures: An REE substrate that generates behavioural diversity only through downstream stochastic / curiosity channels (no proposal-side mechanism) will fail to reproduce the goal-biased trajectory-proposal pattern Pfeiffer & Foster document.

If hippocampal-replay-driven proposal is REDUNDANT in REE (covered by existing CEM trajectory generation + MECH-293 ghost-goal probes), this paper would predict no additional diversity-generation capacity from a separate proposal-side mechanism.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Before goal-directed navigation, the rat hippocampus generates brief place-cell sequences encoding spatial trajectories that progress from current location to known goals. The sequences predict immediate future behaviour and encode novel start-goal combinations.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-065 may include a proposal-side diversity channel via hippocampal-style trajectory-sampling. REE already partially instantiates this through MECH-293 awake ghost-goal probes and the CEM trajectory generator in HippocampalModule. Pfeiffer & Foster supports the architectural commitment that proposal diversity (sampling many candidate trajectories) is a substrate component of behavioural diversity, complementary to action-selection-side stochasticity (MECH-313) and curiosity (MECH-314). Candidate sub-MECH: MECH-315 proposal-diversity-channel.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper documents the existence of forward-trajectory sequences in rats; it does not directly establish that these sequences are the *substrate of behavioural diversity* in the explore-exploit sense. The mapping is plausible and well-rehearsed in the hippocampal-MCTS literature (Mattar & Daw 2018, Pfeiffer 2020 review), but the diversity-generation claim is one inferential step beyond what Pfeiffer & Foster directly demonstrate.
mapping.source_context: Place-cell electrophysiology in freely-moving rats during open-arena goal-directed navigation; Foster lab Johns Hopkins.

confidence_components.notes: Nature venue, foundational. Mapping fidelity moderate because the diversity-generation interpretation is one inferential step beyond the paper's direct claim about goal-biased trajectory sequences.
